skill.name: gws-tasks
skill.description: Use the gws CLI to manage Google Tasks task lists and tasks.
skill.summary: Manage Google Tasks lists and tasks through the Google Workspace CLI.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: The static backtick and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code, a fenced CLI example, and ordinary words in SKILL.md. The skill still has medium operational risk because it instructs agents to run the external gws CLI against authenticated Google Tasks data, including delete, clear, patch, update, and move actions.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Can Modify Authenticated Tasks Data
medium_findings.description: The skill tells agents to use the gws tasks command and documents methods that delete, clear, patch, update, move, and insert Google Tasks data. This appears legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can change or remove user data if a model runs commands without careful confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command reference and destructive method list are explicit in SKILL.md. No malicious intent is shown, but the authenticated CLI actions create real operational risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported many Ruby or shell backtick execution findings. The cited content is Markdown inline code and fenced command documentation, not executable Ruby or shell code inside the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation, and the backticks delimit command examples. I found no Ruby source or shell script execution wrapper in the skill.

low_findings.title: Relative Shared-Skill Reference Is Not Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The '../gws-shared/SKILL.md' reference asks the agent to read shared documentation for authentication, flags, and security rules. It is a relative documentation dependency, not code that reads arbitrary filesystem paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relative path is fixed and points to a sibling shared skill file. No variable path construction or user-controlled filesystem access is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code. The cited lines contain the description text and the normal word 'method' in API documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is no hashing, encryption, or crypto API use in the cited Markdown lines. The alerts appear to be substring matches unrelated to cryptography.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated External Command Usage
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill routes work through the gws CLI and includes commands for inspecting and invoking Google Tasks resources. External command use is expected here, but agents should confirm destructive operations before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CLI usage is explicitly documented and tied to authenticated Google Workspace actions. The pattern is legitimate but can affect user data.

content.user_title: Manage Google Tasks from AI workflows
content.value_statement: Google Tasks work often requires switching between planning and task management tools. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use the gws CLI for task list and task operations.
actual_capabilities: Lists Google Tasks task lists for the authenticated user.
Gets, creates, updates, patches, and deletes task lists.
Lists, gets, creates, updates, patches, moves, clears, and deletes tasks.
Uses gws help output to inspect available resources and methods.
Uses gws schema output to build valid params and json flags.
limitations: Requires the gws CLI to be installed and authenticated.
Depends on the shared Google Workspace skill for auth and security guidance.
Cannot create tasks assigned from Docs or Chat Spaces through the Tasks API.
Destructive operations can delete or hide task data and require user confirmation.

use_cases.title: Organize personal task lists
use_cases.description: Create, rename, and review task lists while planning personal work in an AI chat.

use_cases.title: Maintain project action items
use_cases.description: Add, move, update, and complete tasks across project lists using the authenticated Workspace account.

use_cases.title: Audit task backlog status
use_cases.description: List tasks and task lists before a meeting to identify overdue, hidden, or misplaced work.

prompt_templates.title: List my task lists
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my Google Tasks task lists. Use gws tasks help first if you need the exact command.

prompt_templates.title: Create a planning list
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Google Tasks list named Weekly Planning, then show me the new list details before adding tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Update project tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Find my Project Launch task list, add three tasks from this meeting summary, and ask before changing existing tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Reorganize a task backlog
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Engineering Backlog tasks, propose a new order, and wait for approval before moving or deleting anything.

output_examples.input: List my Google Tasks task lists.
output_examples.output: Found task lists for the authenticated account, including Personal, Work, and Project Launch. No changes were made.

output_examples.input: Add follow-up tasks from this meeting to Project Launch.
output_examples.output: Added three tasks to Project Launch: send recap, confirm launch date, and assign QA owner.

output_examples.input: Clear completed tasks from my Work list.
output_examples.output: The Work list has completed tasks that can be hidden by clear. I will wait for confirmation before running that action.

best_practices: Inspect gws schema before building params or json flags for a method.
Confirm list and task identifiers before update, move, clear, or delete actions.
Summarize planned changes before running commands that modify Google Tasks data.
anti_patterns: Do not delete or clear tasks without explicit user confirmation.
Do not assume task list names are unique without checking returned identifiers.
Do not bypass the shared Google Workspace authentication and security guidance.

faq.question: What does this skill manage?
faq.answer: It manages Google Tasks task lists and tasks through the gws CLI.

faq.question: Does it require Google authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. The gws CLI must be installed and authenticated for the target Google account.

faq.question: Can it delete task data?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented API methods include delete and clear actions, so users should confirm destructive changes.

faq.question: Can it manage assigned tasks from Docs or Chat Spaces?
faq.answer: It can interact with some assigned task records, but new assigned tasks must be created from the assignment surface.

faq.question: How should Claude or Codex discover valid parameters?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents to run gws schema for the specific tasks resource and method.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk notice for authenticated external commands and destructive Google Tasks operations.
